messages.content:
Which of the following is an example of a design guideline for HCI?


a.
Using a small font type for text

b.
Providing clear feedback for user actions


c.
Placing important information in hard-to-find locations


d.
Overloading a screen with too much information
b. Providing clear feedback for user actions

Which of the following UI principles emphasizes the importance of uniformity throughout an interface?



a.
Affordance


b.
Mapping


c.
Consistency


d.
Feedback
c. Consistency

Which of the following is an example of a visual hierarchy in UI design?



a.
Using bold text for all headings


b.
Using a monochromatic color scheme


c.
Placing important information at the bottom of a page


d.
Using a larger font size for more important information
d. Using a larger font size for more important information

Which of the following UI principles emphasizes the importance of providing clear and concise response to the user?



a.
Affordance


b.
Consistency


c.
Mapping


d.
Feedback
d. Feedback

Which of the following is an example of the principle of affordance in UI design?


a.
Using a simple and intuitive navigation menu


b.
Providing a hover effect for clickable elements


c.
Placing a large, red button in the center of the screen


d.
Using a green color scheme for all buttons
b. Providing a hover effect for clickable elements

Which of the following is an example of the principle of mapping in UI design?



a.
Providing a clear indication of the current page or section


b.
Placing the "back" button in the top left corner of the screen


c.
Using a color scheme that matches the company's branding


d.
Using a consistent font size throughout the interface
b. Placing the "back" button in the top left corner of the screen

Which of the following UI principles emphasizes the use of visual cues to indicate the function of interface elements?


a.
Feedback


b.
Consistency


c.
Affordance


d.
Mapping
c. Affordance

……………………………. is the process of designing the interactions between humans and technology


a.
GUI design


b.
Interface design


c.
Interaction design


d.
Display design
c. Interaction design

Select the goal of interaction design

I. Usable

II. Useful

III. Desirable

IV. Accessible


a.
III and IV


b.
I,II and III


c.
I, II, III and IV


d.
I, II and IV
c. I, II, III and IV

Which of the following is an example of an output device?


a.
Mouse


b.
Keyboard


c.
Printer


d.
Scanner
c. Printer

Which of the following is NOT a main goal of HCI?



a.
To reduce error


b.
To enhance user satisfaction


c.
To increase security


d.
To improve efficiency
c. To increase security

Which of the following is a MAIN challenge in HCI?



a.
Designing for diversity


b.
Emphasizing speed over accuracy


c.
Maximizing user frustration


d.
Creating complex interfaces
a. Designing for diversity

Which of the following is an example of a user interface?



a.
CSS


b.
JavaScript


c.
Phyton


d.
HTML
d. HTML

Which of the following is NOT a factor that affects user satisfaction in HCI?



a.
Usability


b.
Cost


c.
Aesthetic


d.
Performance
b. Cost

Which of the following is NOT a main component of HCI?



a.
Ethics


b.
Computer


c.
Interaction


d.
Human
b. Computer

Which of the following UI principles emphasizes the importance of providing clear and concise response to the user?



a.
Affordance


b.
Consistency


c.
Mapping


d.
Feedback
d. Feedback

Which of the following is NOT a main component of HCI?



a.
Ethics


b.
Computer


c.
Interaction


d.
Human
b. Computer
